political.s: No significant political donations or lobbying activity found in available public records. As a financial services and automotive company focused on e-commerce, Vroom has maintained a minimal political profile.

charity.s: No substantial corporate charitable giving programs documented. The company has not publicly disclosed major philanthropic initiatives or charitable partnerships.

environment.s: Company previously stated commitment to incorporating electric and hybrid vehicles and partnering with eco-friendly delivery companies, though these initiatives were abandoned following 2024 operational shutdown. Environmental sustainability efforts were limited.

labor.s: Company laid off 800 employees in January 2024 following closure of e-commerce operations. Prior layoffs included 25% reduction in 2018. Glassdoor ratings show mixed employee satisfaction at 3.7/5 for compensation and benefits.

dei.s: No substantial DEI initiatives or public commitments documented. Company maintained standard hiring and employment practices without notable diversity programs.

animals.s: Not applicable - automotive and financial services company with no animal testing or animal-related business operations.

guns.s: Not applicable - company operates in automotive e-commerce and financial services, not firearms manufacturing or sales.

privacy.s: Company privacy policies collect personal information including name, email, photographs and vehicle information. Data sharing practices include vendors and service providers. FTC settlement in 2024 required $1 million refund for failing to provide required disclosures.

execPay.s: CEO Paul Hennessy had $500,000 base salary with 100% bonus target as of 2020. Limited recent compensation disclosure available. Average employee salary approximately $111k per year.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
